title: An Examination into Star Ratings Based on the Official Yelp Dataset

markdown.headingText: An Examination into Star Ratings Based on the Official Yelp Dataset
markdown.markdown:
```{=html}
```

MSc Business Analytics · Business Analytics Project · Ariane Damian · Dec. 02, 2025

--- ## Introduction Reputation is a direct reflection of a business' performance, influencing consumer behaviour through perceived image, reliability, and quality. With the proliferation of technology, online review platforms have become essential for determining business reputation — and Yelp is one of the most established. This project analyses the official Yelp dataset to: - Understand the distribution of businesses, star ratings, and reviews across the platform - Identify key attributes and characteristics that influence star ratings - Apply and evaluate regression models to predict star ratings - Propose a new analytics-based business model for Yelp centred on Star Rating Prediction --- ## Methodology ### Data Preparation - Raw dataset consisted of approximately **150,000 rows** and **14 columns** - Missing and null values were removed - Columns *hours*, *address*, and *postal_code* were excluded as irrelevant to the target variable - *Categories* and *Attributes* were split by frequency into **12 key categories** and **33 key attributes** - Final cleaned dataset: **150,243 rows** and **56 columns** ### Modelling Techniques Three regression models were applied to predict Star Rating: | Model | Purpose | |--------|---------| | Linear Regression (LR) | Baseline model; explores possible linear relationships between variables and star rating | | Random Forest Regression (RFR) | Handles non-linear relationships; robust to outliers and missing data | | Gradient Boosting Regression (GBR) | Advanced model; iteratively corrects errors to maximise predictive accuracy | ## Summary of Findings ### Exploratory Data Analysis Patterns in business distribution, star ratings, and attributes were examined across the dataset. | Finding | Key Observation | |---|---| | **Business Categories** | *Food & Restaurants* dominates with ~65,000 businesses — a heavily saturated market | | **Star Ratings** | Most businesses rated between 3–4.5 stars; 4 stars is the most common | | **Category vs. Rating** | Most categories cluster near the 3.6 average — the individual business, not its category, drives rating | | **Review Count** | Most businesses receive very few reviews; review count does not significantly correlate with star rating | | **Business Attributes** | Most attributes (e.g. outdoor seating, delivery, bike parking), when present, are associated with higher ratings | ### Other relevant findings include - The market for *Pets* and *Hotels/Travel* categories is less saturated, offering greater opportunity for smaller businesses to build customer relationships - Low review counts may indicate low visibility or neutral satisfaction — both addressable through Yelp's advertising and analytics services - Growing the review sample size by increasing customer engagement would also improve the accuracy of the Star Rating Prediction model over time --- ### Regression Analysis All three models were evaluated using R², Mean Absolute Error (MAE), and Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE). | Model | Performance Summary | |---|---| | **Linear Regression** | Weakest performer; lowest R², highest MAE & RMSE; ~1 star average deviation | | **Random Forest** | Slightly better overall; still approximately 1 star deviation across both datasets | | **Gradient Boosting** | Marginal improvements in some metrics; not decisively better than RFR | - **Random Forest Regression** was identified as the most robust model, though differences between models were marginal ::: {.callout-important} All models are limited to business-level data; incorporating **review text sentiment** and **pricing information** would likely improve predictions substantially. ::: ## Recommendations The following improvements and opportunities are recommended for Yelp and future model development: - Develop a **premium Star Rating Prediction service** that businesses can subscribe to for performance forecasting and improvement guidance - Use Star Rating predictions **internally** to refine Yelp's ranking algorithms and "Best of Yelp" promotional lists - Incorporate **review text sentiment analysis** and **price-level data** to significantly improve model accuracy - Expand modelling to address **class imbalance** in ratings (few low-rated businesses) and **category imbalance** (dominance of food businesses) - Offer **targeted advertising services** to businesses with low review counts to increase visibility and grow the dataset ```{=html}
```
